channel.title: Synchronization on Kevin wang's blog
channel.description: Recent content in Synchronization on Kevin wang's blog

item.title: Concurrency Synchronization
item.description: On shared-memory machines, threads communicate by reading and writing shared data structures. Simple updates use hardware atomics; larger critical sections use synchronization constructs — either blocking (deschedule the waiter) or busy-wait (spin until a condition holds). This post covers the busy-wait building blocks: spin locks for mutual exclusion and barriers for rendezvous.
